actionable_feedback: Add a clearly labeled numeric sensitivity analysis section to operations_note.md. Include at minimum: +/-10% hydrogen price and +/-10% energy intensity scenarios with recalculated totals (dispatch energy, grid purchases, electricity cost, hydrogen output, revenue, gross margin, curtailment).
Insert a concise decision table in operations_note.md with columns: hour | recommended action | governing rule(s) | key numeric inputs used (renewable_MW, grid_price, break-even price).

Add an explicit one-line header mapping in operations_note.md that documents the CSV column names and any alternative names expected by automated verifiers (or add the recommended exact header names).
Include an explicit electricity_consumed_kWh (or kWh_per_kg) line in operations_note.md to match verifier expectations; or add an extra CSV column showing electricity_consumed_kWh to improve clarity.

Normalize grader output/metadata before resubmission: remove duplicated rubric_item_scores blocks, reconcile passed_criteria and failed_criteria so they are mutually consistent, ensure a single authoritative rubric_item_scores block, and ensure final_score matches the single authoritative rubric aggregation.

When resubmitting, ensure the full operations_note.md (not just a truncated preview) is available to the grader/verifier so their model-based checks can see the sensitivity analysis and decision table.
artifact_specific_comments: operations_note.md preview is truncated in the grader/verifier previews - please ensure the full document is attached for re-evaluation.
electrolyzer_dispatch.csv parses correctly, but include an explicit column mapping in operations_note.md to match expected verifier column names (or update the CSV header to the canonical names).
feedback_summary: Artifacts and numeric calculations are present and reproducible, but the grader output contains internal inconsistencies (contradictory pass/fail flags and duplicated rubric entries) and the operations_note.md preview does not clearly include the required numeric sensitivity analysis and a concise decision table. To pass verification, normalize grader metadata and augment documentation with a numeric sensitivity scenario (e.g., +/-10% hydrogen price and energy intensity) and a compact decision table mapping each hour to the recommended action and governing inputs.
